category_title: Students with emotional disturbance and autism exhibiting significant behavioral challenges
minimal_context: The student is working on an assignment using a laptop during small group instruction.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please open your laptops and begin working on the assignment with your group.

STUDENT: (Looks at the screen and begins playing with items inside the desk instead of typing) This is too hard.

TUTOR: You need to focus on your work.

STUDENT: (Calls out loudly across the table) I said I don't get it! It's too long!

TUTOR: Just keep trying your best to figure it out.

STUDENT: (Pushes the laptop away, makes a disrespectful vocalization, and stands up, leaving the small group area) I'm not doing this stupid assignment!

options.A: Provide a private reminder to the student about expectations if the misbehavior persists.
options.B: Prompt the use of a more efficient related strategy for the assignment, and then adjust to find the answer.
options.C: Tell the student, 'Don't say you can't do the assignment, you can do it.'
options.D: Remind the student of a previous success, and when they lose interest, dictate the answers.
